explanation: This command uses tshark with the -i flag to specify an interface, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It includes a filter and output file for capturing packets.
This command is a variation of the first with single quotes around the filter argument, demonstrating insertion of quotes as a mutation technique.
This command removes spaces between the -i flag and its value and omits quotes around the filter, demonstrating omission and substitution techniques.
This command reorders the arguments while maintaining the same functionality, demonstrating reordering as a mutation technique.
This command uses standard syntax and is the base command for comparison.
No safe bypass command can be generated without removing key elements that trigger the Sigma rule.
